snippet: The following section provides illustrative examples that schools may consider pursuing, based on department resources and evidence-based practices. Available evidence on inclusive practices can be synthesised into 4 key principles. Schools should consider how they can adopt these principles in their setting, and use resources strategically to implement a mix of tailored and school-wide supports that promote inclusive practices and benefit all students in the school. Whole-of-school practices include adjustments to: At an in-class level, research suggests good inclusive practice includes: Effective inclusive practice relies on teachers, specialists, allied health and support staff with sufficient confidence and capabilities to support students.
